title: The Coming Age
subtitle: The inevitability of the 4th Industrial Revolution and a pathway foreword
author: americanambition
raw_article:
The world is looking down the barrel of a gun. The 4th Industrial Revolution is fast approaching, with rapid shifts predicted within the next 3-8 years. The question is no longer whether this change can be stopped, but how it can be used for our benefit. The tiger is in the room, and we must ride it to survive.

Thanks for reading American Ambition! Subscribe for free to receive new posts and support my work.

What Lies Ahead

In his book the 4th Industrial Revolution, Klaus Schwab lays out the future of the world he and his compatriots desire to achieve. While the book was published in 2017, we can clearly see the predictions in this book are quickly becoming reality. The development of AI, 3D printing, quantum computing, Meta, and many other technologies are beginning to leave their infancy and find themselves in our every day lives. The globalization project is speeding ahead as we become further interconnected, especially due to the pandemic.

Shared understanding is particularly critical if we are to shape a collective future that reflects common objectives and values. We must have a comprehensive and globally shared view of how technology is changing our lives and those of future generations, and how it is reshaping the economic, social, cultural and human context in which we live.

In order for this industrialization to take place in accordance with the World Economic Forum’s (WEC) vision, it must be a cooperative and cohesive effort among various groups of power across the globe.

Shaping the fourth industrial revolution to ensure that it is empowering and human-centred, rather than divisive and dehumanizing, is not a task for any single stakeholder or sector or for any one region, industry or culture. The fundamental and global nature of this revolution means it will affect and be influenced by all countries, economies, sectors and people. It is, therefore, critical that we invest attention and energy in multistakeholder cooperation across academic, social, political, national and industry boundaries. These interactions and collaborations are needed to create positive, common and hope-filled narratives, enabling individuals and groups from all parts of the world to participate in, and benefit from, the ongoing transformations.

This Revolution will be a radical reshaping of every aspect of life into a new society built around Economic streamlining and automation, radical egalitarianism (excluding the elites, naturally), and the beginning of the transhumanist age. It will not only be a political and economic upheaval, but it will be a spiritual one as well.

The reality of disruption and the inevitability of the impact it will have on us does not mean that we are powerless in face of it. It is our responsibility to ensure that we establish a set of common values to drive policy choices and to enact the changes that will make the fourth industrial revolution an opportunity for all.

Diverging Paths

Now what are we to do in the face of this looming future? For the nationalists and the populists of various stripes, the consensus seems to be that we must stop this globalization at all costs. We must fight to retain our national sovereignty and self determination, resisting the encroachment in all sectors of this globohomo menace. While this idealism is endearing and makes sense to most people, I would like to propose a return to an earlier line of Anglo thought.

Empire Eternal

What if I told you Karl Schwab was right?

This 4th Industrial Revolution is something inevitable. Politics is a zero-sum game, especially at a global scale. We are in a race not only with foreign political powers, but with our own domestic enemies, all vying for control of the game board. The current right can grandstand all it wants about taking the high road and resisting the plan of the WEC, but their solution is one of stagnation which will lead to ruin.

If we do not progress, our enemies will. Politics is power, and whoever wields the power wields the outcome of these unstoppable forces. We should not fear the coming revolution, but rather be the driving force in assurance of our national interests. The WEC to be sure is a wicked organization with a hatred of the West and America in particular, but we cannot ignore what the globalists recognize as the inevitable future. America, even in its current state, is still the world superpower and thus stands in the way of the globalist elite. Europe for the most part has fallen, and America exists as the last bastion of hope against those who seek to reorient society into an eternal fiefdom built on a genetically modified slave class of third world immigrants. The American middle class implicitly recognizes this I believe, which explains the rise of Donald Trump as an elite who genuinely cares about the subjects of his country. We see this same sentiment with Fox News host Tucker Carlson, who is a remnant of the aristocratic WASP elite who once ran this nation and brought it to its peak of glory.

American hegemony today seems to be something lamented by the modern American nationalist as a mistake; a grave moral error littered with abuses and an affront to the national sovereignty of other nations. This argument is easy to make in the wake of the forever wars, which not only left various power vacuums in its wake, but claimed thousands of American lives in the process. However, the consequences here seem to be minute compared to the coming apocalypse of the globalist Great Reset. We need elites who desire the Pax Americana to keep its global status in the face of the growing influence of foreign nations as well as the multinational corporations and alliances currently being used by the globalists.

Final Thoughts

Power vacuums will always be filled, and the question is whether it will be by America or our enemies. The proponents of the Great Reset recognize this, and have been working tirelessly to conquer western civilization. In the race to the future, America must see itself as the geopolitical savior. We must again manifest destiny in the face of those who seek to destroy us, and unite the collapsing West under are glorious Stars and Stripes. This has been the destiny of all great WASP nations, with their glorious empires ruling the world in their due seasons. This is not something to lament as evil, but to embrace as our birthright. It is the power of America which the globalist fears, because it is the only thing which can resist its agenda. We cannot stop the tides of change, but we can secure our place as the victors of the age to come.

Thanks for reading American Ambition! Subscribe for free to receive new posts and support my work.

title: The Baptist Question
subtitle: A defense of the baptist faith against the accusations of liberalism
author: americanambition
raw_article:
A recent controversy has come to a head in the Christian political sphere. While the accusations being levied against the Baptists are nothing new, this iteration seems to have caused a lot of ruckus, spurring conversations on the correlation between baptist theology and individualistic liberalism. One such conversation occurred on the Ars Politica podcast episode The Religion Of American Greatness.

In discussing this book, the following challenge was issued at 1:16:00

Is there an essential connection between baptist theology and liberalism?

This is an incredibly important question. Are Baptists just the theological symptom of hyper-individualism? Does baptist theology lend itself to these views? In order for us to come to a conclusion, we must first examine what baptist theology is, specifically it’s covenant theology.

1689 Federalism Crash Course

Baptist covenant theology, called 1689 Federalism, differs from its Westminster counterpart in important ways. First, the covenant of grace is not present in the old covenant. Instead, it is a promise progressively revealed in the Old Testament, with all of those who had faith in the promise receiving a retroactive application of Christ’s atonement. Second, within the Abrahamic promise there are two seeds, the physical and spiritual descendants of Abraham. The physical descendants of Abraham were established into a geopolitical nation which eventually crumbled because of their disobedience, while the spiritual descendants were born again into an eternal, spiritual kingdom with its citizenship brought about through imputed righteousness. This is presented by Paul in Galatians 4:21-31, by contrasting the physical seed, Ishmael, with the spiritual seed, Isaac. Both were circumcised in the flesh, but only one was circumcised in the heart. The outward sign of circumcision was a sign of obedience and setting apart for the physical promises of Israel. Baptism in this way is similar to circumcision, in which it sets apart the people in the covenant of grace from the people of the world. However, the covenant of grace is not a mixed covenant, containing believers and unbelievers like the old. It is a perfect covenant. It is for this reason that we do not baptize infants on the basis of the faith of one or more believing parents. The merit of a parent cannot be transferred to a child, and baptism is the sign and symbol of a perfect covenant made up of the faithful in Christ. While this is a very scant explanation of this subject, I hope it has given the reader somewhat of an understanding into the baptist mind.

Baptism and the Two Kingdoms

Now how does baptist covenant theology provide an answer to the challenge issued? It must be understood within the context of two kingdoms theology. Man is born into sin under the federal headship of Adam. He is a member of mankind, born into familial relationships and hierarchy of which he did not choose, but was established as the norm by God at the foundation of the world. The liberal order due to sin denies these truths and seeks to apply individualism to every aspect of life. Paedobaptists leverage that because Baptists believe that one must be able to express ones faith prior to baptism unlike in the previous covenant, it is no different than the liberal choosing their social and familial connections in the world of man. This is a mixing up of the two kingdoms. In the kingdom of man there are relationships one is born into, and in the spiritual kingdom one is born into bondage because of Adam’s sin. The federal headship of Christ does not effect the familial relationships of the physical, but effects the spiritual state of man. If one were to contest this point, they must wrestle with Galatians 3:28, in which all these physical relationships and distinctions they seek to protect must be abolished.

Final Thoughts

“In those days they shall no longer say: ‘The fathers have eaten sour grapes, and the children's teeth are set on edge.’ But everyone shall die for his own sin. Each man who eats sour grapes, his teeth shall be set on edge’” (Jeremiah 31:29-30

In the new covenant, each person is responsible for their own sin before God. The covenant status of the father and mother is not the status of their children, and cannot be merited for them. They can be raised and instructed in the way they should go, but it is the Father who calls into Christ through the Spirit. The claim that baptist theology leads to societal liberalism is one which confuses the physical and the spiritual kingdoms and makes covenant status something inherited, not an adoption, contra scripture.

Thank you for reading my first substack post. Looking forward to writing more and developing the art!

-AC

Thanks for reading American Ambition! Subscribe for free to receive new posts and support my work.

Thanks for reading American Ambition! Subscribe for free to receive new posts and support my work.

title: Coming soon
author: americanambition

This is American Ambition, a newsletter about politics, theology, philosophy, and reshaping the American Empire.

Subscribe now
